summary: Reference matrix for precedence, chaining, interpolation, and compound-expression rules.

cases.upstream_intent: Operator precedence should remain stable when arithmetic appears inside larger expressions.
cases.zr_contract: Arithmetic precedence is preserved before call lowering and before member-chain binding.
assertions: AST nesting matches operator precedence
compiled opcode order preserves grouping
runtime value matches expected result

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Member access, call, and index chaining require an observable evaluation order contract.
cases.zr_contract: Member, call, and index chains lower left-to-right without hidden regrouping.
cases.expected_outcome: mixed member-call-index chains execute in the expected order
assertions: AST preserves left-associated chain nodes
lowered instructions keep chain order
runtime side effects show left-to-right evaluation

return: mixed member-call-index chains execute in the expected order

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Malformed chains should fail as syntax errors instead of drifting into unrelated parse states.
cases.zr_contract: Invalid tokens after a call or member separator produce targeted expression diagnostics.
cases.expected_outcome: broken chain syntax fails with expression.chain diagnostic
assertions: diagnostic points at the separator context
AST is not partially reinterpreted as a different construct
artifact generation is skipped

exception: broken chain syntax fails with expression.chain diagnostic

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Nested conditional syntax needs dedicated negative coverage.
cases.zr_contract: Missing operands in conditional expressions fail in parsing before lowering.
cases.expected_outcome: conditional expression with a missing branch is rejected
assertions: diagnostic family names the conditional expression
line and column isolate the missing branch site
no fallback null operand is synthesized

exception: conditional expression with a missing branch is rejected

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Deeply nested conditional expressions need explicit associativity boundaries.
cases.zr_contract: Nested conditional expressions retain deterministic associativity and branch grouping.
cases.expected_outcome: nested conditionals parse and execute with stable grouping
assertions: AST nesting depth is asserted
branch expressions remain distinct nodes
runtime result matches the expected branch

return: nested conditionals parse and execute with stable grouping

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Embedded expressions must not leak delimiters or precedence outside interpolation regions.
cases.zr_contract: Interpolation boundaries isolate embedded expressions while still honoring normal precedence inside the embedded region.
cases.expected_outcome: interpolation with nested expressions preserves inner precedence and outer literal slices
assertions: literal and expression segments remain separate
embedded AST honors normal precedence rules
runtime string result is stable

return: interpolation with nested expressions preserves inner precedence and outer literal slices

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Mixed chains combine lookup, call, and closure capture pressures in the same expression.
cases.zr_contract: Member, index, and call links compose without forcing one syntax family to masquerade as another.
cases.expected_outcome: mixed chains keep separate member and index semantics while remaining executable
assertions: member and index nodes stay distinct in AST
compiled instructions use the correct op families
runtime result preserves both lookup paths

return: mixed chains keep separate member and index semantics while remaining executable

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Compound assignment interacting with chains needs its own lowering coverage.
cases.zr_contract: Compound assignment respects chain resolution order and does not duplicate receiver evaluation.
cases.expected_outcome: compound assignment over a chained receiver updates exactly one target
assertions: receiver side effect happens once
opcode sequence preserves read-modify-write shape
runtime value reflects a single update

return: compound assignment over a chained receiver updates exactly one target

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Expression refactors often regress closure capture and chain ordering together.
cases.zr_contract: Captured locals referenced inside chained expressions retain lexical binding and evaluation order.
cases.expected_outcome: captured local reads inside chains remain stable across compiler refactors
assertions: captured slot binding is stable
lowered chain still references the captured local
runtime result reflects lexical capture

return: captured local reads inside chains remain stable across compiler refactors

cases.upstream_intent: Evaluation order regressions are hard to spot without dedicated side-effectful chains.
cases.zr_contract: Argument and receiver evaluation order stays deterministic for chained calls.
cases.expected_outcome: side effects in chained calls preserve stable ordering
assertions: compiled argument evaluation order is fixed
artifact text shows the expected call sequence
runtime side effects match the expected log

return: side effects in chained calls preserve stable ordering
cleanup_trace: compiled argument evaluation order is fixed

cases.upstream_intent: Lua-specific call sugar should not be imported accidentally through chain lowering.
cases.zr_contract: ZR does not accept Lua table-call or string-call sugar outside its explicit syntax.
cases.expected_outcome: Lua-only call sugar is rejected as unsupported syntax
assertions: diagnostic points at the sugar form
parser does not reinterpret the construct as a normal call
project compile path rejects it identically

exception: Lua-only call sugar is rejected as unsupported syntax

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Python comparison chaining has distinct semantics that should not leak into ZR.
cases.zr_contract: ZR comparison operators do not auto-chain into Python-style multi-comparison semantics.
cases.expected_outcome: comparison chaining is either rejected or parsed as ordinary binary grouping according to ZR rules
assertions: grammar does not create implicit comparison-chain nodes
compiler keeps binary comparison semantics only
runtime or diagnostics match the documented ZR rule

exception: comparison chaining is either rejected or parsed as ordinary binary grouping according to ZR rules

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary
